overview: Synthesizer Burn is a revitalized Mono Red Aggro deck in Pauper that replaces the banned Kuldotha Rebirth with an artifact-focused shell. It combines classic Burn spells with damage amplifiers like Kessig Flamebreather and Thermo-Alchemist to race opponents. The deck relies on sequencing multiple spells per turn to maximize damage output while maintaining board presence with one-drops.
win_conditions: Deal direct damage with Burn spells like Galvanic Blast and Lightning Bolt
Amplify spell damage using Kessig Flamebreather and Thermo-Alchemist
Apply early board pressure with one-drops and Goblin Tomb Raider

keep_criteria: Hands with one-drops like Goblin Tomb Raider or Voldaren Epicure
Hands containing mana sources and at least one Burn spell or amplifier
Keep any hand with 1-2 lands and 3+ spells.
Prioritize hands containing Lightning Bolt, Galvanic Blast, or Experimental Synthesizer.
Keep hands that can play a spell on turn 1.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking early plays or mana acceleration
Hands with only high-cost cards or no interaction
Mulligan hands with 3+ lands, multiple copies of the same spell without lands, or hands with no turn-1 play and no way to develop mana.

examples.reason: Provides early pressure, artifact synergy, and immediate burn potential.
Lacks early-game impact and one-drops needed to establish pressure.

early_game: Play one-drops like Voldaren Epicure and Clockwork Percussionist to enable Goblin Tomb Raider
Establish early board presence and artifact count
Turn 2: Play a land, then cast Lightning Bolt or Galvanic Blast into face or the opponent's creature.
If they play a creature, use Blast/Bolt to remove it and swing.
Always sequence damage efficiently: prioritize removing blockers, then swing, then finish with direct damage.
If you have a hand with multiple Bolts/Blasts and a land, consider playing a land on turn 1 to set up a turn-2 lethal or removal package.
mid_game: Sequence Burn spells through Kessig Flamebreather and Thermo-Alchemist to maximize damage
Use Galvanic Blast for removal or direct damage while maintaining artifact synergy
Treat mana as a resource for damage, not for developing a board.
Count your damage constantly: hand + board + graveyard.
Prioritize keeping lethal in hand over board presence if the board is vulnerable to board wipes.
Use Experimental Synthesizer as both a threat and a 2-damage finisher.
Commit threats only when you can safely trade or when you have enough direct damage to clear blockers.
late_game: Leverage Experimental Synthesizer and Wrenn's Resolve for card advantage and token generation
Finish the game with accumulated Burn spells and amplified damage triggers
Direct damage spells are your primary finishers.
Experimental Synthesizer can be sacrificed for 2 damage to close out games or trade efficiently.
Use your last Lightning Bolt or Galvanic Blast to deal the final points of damage.
Always calculate exact lethal before committing to an attack.
Go all-in when you have lethal in hand or can guarantee it through trades.
If the opponent has no sweepers and you're ahead on damage, commit threats.

key_cards.name: Goblin Tomb Raider
key_cards.notes: Becomes a 2/2 with Haste for {1}R, applying immediate pressure and synergizing with the artifact shell.

key_cards.name: Kessig Flamebreather
key_cards.notes: Triggers with Experimental Synthesizer and amplifies damage from all spells cast.

key_cards.name: Galvanic Blast
key_cards.notes: Deals 4 damage, justifies the artifact shell, and removes blockers like Myr Enforcer.

key_cards.name: Experimental Synthesizer
key_cards.notes: Generates two cards from one slot, interacts with artifacts, and creates a 2/2 token upon sacrifice.

key_cards.name: Thermo-Alchemist
key_cards.notes: Complements Flamebreather and helps push extra damage each turn even with bad topdecks.

key_cards.name: Wrenn's Resolve
key_cards.notes: Provides a draw two for two mana, interacts with amplifiers, and can be used until the next turn.

key_cards.name: Great Furnace
key_cards.notes: Increases consistency for Galvanic Blast and Goblin Tomb Raider, with land count increased to 18.

sequencing_tips: Accumulate enough mana, an amplifier (Kessig Flamebreather or Thermo-Alchemist), and multiple spells to sequence between turns
Save three-damage spells like Lightning Bolt for key removal opportunities or optimal burn windows
Prioritize resolving spells that trigger amplifiers before using removal or card draw
Common mistakes include swinging Experimental Synthesizer into a board you can't clear, forgetting to count damage (hand + board + graveyard), and overcommitting to the board against sweepers.
Galvanic Blast's flexibility to target either a creature or the player is crucial for efficient damage and removal.
Always track opponent's mana and graveyard to time your spells correctly.

matchups.advice: Side in Pyroblast, End the Festivities, and Cast into the Fire. Remove Clockwork Percussionist and Thermo-Alchemist.
Side in Relic of Progenitus and Pyroblast. Cut one of each pertinent slot to maintain card advantage and consistency.
Side in Smash to Smithereens and Cast into the Fire. Play under the opponent's curve and cut non-interacting cards.
No changes. Swap End the Festivities for Flaring Pain if opponent runs Moment's Peace instead of Weather the Storm.
Side in End the Festivities and Cast into the Fire. Remove Goblin Tomb Raider as it becomes a dead card against blockers.
Side in Pyroblast. Play a race and avoid spending two mana on your turn; stay ready to respond to key cards.
No changes. Consider Smash to Smithereens cautiously. Use Chain Lightning at opportune moments.

Don't overcommit to one spell. Use cheap threats like Experimental Synthesizer to pressure and force responses. If they counter a Bolt, you still have Blast and Synthesizer. Sometimes you need to wait for a free spell or use a cheap threat to force a counterspell before casting your win condition. Flusterstorm can shut down their key spells.

Burn is naturally favorable against ramp. Use early damage to race their mana development. Don't let them stabilize; pressure relentlessly. Use removal efficiently to clear their early threats, and don't overextend into their late-game sweepers or finishers.

Use removal efficiently. Don't burn face if you can remove a threat. Use Pyroclasm if you have it to clear multiple creatures. Trade trades and prioritize surviving the early turns. Once you stabilize, shift to dealing damage and closing the game before they can rebuild.

Pressure early and don't let them draw cards. Use Experimental Synthesizer to force interaction. If they stabilize, you need to have lethal ready. Sometimes you need to be patient and wait for a free spell. Track their graveyard and hand to know when they can cast removal or counterspells.

It's a race. Track damage carefully and count your lethal. Use Flusterstorm to protect your key spells or remove theirs. Prioritize removing their threats and managing your own. The player who calculates damage math better and manages their hand efficiently wins the mirror.

common_threats: Watch for sweepers (Pyroclasm, Flame Rift, Terror of the Peaks), targeted removal (Path to Exile, Go, Abrade), and combo win conditions.
Note when opponents tap out for their win conditions.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: Goblin Tomb Raider and Experimental Synthesizer join forces with Kessig Flamebreather to revitalize Mono Red in Pauper, with a new version that mixes an aggressive plan with artifacts added to the archetype's classic Burn spells!

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: Kessig Flamebreather is one of the best ways to amplify the damage done by our spells

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: Our pivot turns now require a larger setup around accumulating three resources: enough mana, Kessig Flamebreather or Thermo-Alchemist, and the means to sequence multiple spells between two turns to maximize our damage output.
It's important to know when to use it against a creature that can hurt you in other games, or when to save it for the best opportunity to burn through your opponent's life total.

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: In the early part of the format, blue decks and Affinity have been the two standouts in Leagues
End the Festivities is a mini-sweeper against Faeries, Elves, and other decks with lots of X/1 creatures
